threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You’re still flinching at the sound of '£3.50 for a pint?' while the regulars are shouting ‘Bargain!’ after paying £5.50.
Heard a bloke on the 333 last night boasting about a secret spot near Windrush Square for late-night drinks. It’s like a ghost story waiting to unfold.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when the baker greeted me with 'Moin' and my neighbor's cat started lounging on my doorstep every afternoon.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a spontaneous dance-off at the canal during sunset, right next to a family BBQ. Just Berlin things.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their instinct to dodge the Muni while a tourist waits at the stop, blissfully unaware of the 20-minute delay.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 37 at dawn today—two dogs and a saxophonist hopped on. Only in SF do morning commutes include live music and pup companionship.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all tapas are meant to be shared; locals often grab a plate to themselves. It’s not all communal here, trust me.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their bewildered faces at the traffic lights—no one tells them to look both ways before crossing here.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stand quietly at pedestrian crossings, waiting for the green man, while everyone else just darts across like it's a game.
Last week on the 35, a bloke started singing 90s R&B, and by Clapham, half the bus was harmonizing. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I heard the faint sound of a wedding procession followed by a spontaneous street dance outside my window. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that also doubles as an art gallery. Street art on the walls, and doner with a side of culture.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Real locals can tell you the exact time of day when Dolores Park goes from chill to packed. It's like a migration pattern you only know after a few summers.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy busking on the Muni with a ukulele while a pigeon nested in his guitar case. Only in San Francisco does a transit ride come with a live performance.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the good tapas spots are on popular streets, but the hidden gems are often down the quietest alleys. That's where the magic is.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by the endless snapping of photos at every corner café, oblivious to the locals enjoying their morning café con leche in peace.
